title: clematis pseudoatragene subsp. pseudoalpina
intro: Clematis pseudoatragene subsp. pseudoalpina is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its delicate, pale yellow flowers and twining stems. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its roots and leaves, which are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clematis pseudoatragene subsp. pseudoalpina include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing its roots and leaves into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
